---
title: "ISLP Labs"
---

A Quarto website that hosts handouts for the labs in
*[An Introduction to Statistical Learning with Applications in Python](https://www.statlearning.com)*
(ISLP), starting with **Chapter 7 — Non-Linear Modeling**. The handouts
re-organize each lab as a sequence of exercises with hide-able
solutions, so that students can attempt the steps themselves before
revealing the worked code.

The lab content is adapted with permission from the source available at
<https://www.statlearning.com>, and tracks the upstream
[`intro-stat-learning/ISLP_labs`](https://github.com/intro-stat-learning/ISLP_labs)
repository.

## Available handouts

- [Chapter 7 — Non-Linear Modeling](Ch07-nonlin-lab.qmd)

## R alternative

Students who prefer to work in R may follow the corresponding chapter of
Emil Hvitfeldt's [ISLR tidymodels labs](https://emilhvitfeldt.github.io/ISLR-tidymodels-labs/07-moving-beyond-linearity.html).

## Original labs

The full set of original ISLP labs (Chapters 2–13) is available as
Jupyter notebooks in the
[`intro-stat-learning/ISLP_labs`](https://github.com/intro-stat-learning/ISLP_labs)
repository.

## Building the handouts

Render the website (default, shows both exercise prompts and solutions):

```bash
quarto render
```

Render a single handout as a *student worksheet* (prompts only, no
solutions):

```bash
quarto render Ch07-nonlin-lab.qmd --profile assign
```

Render a single handout as a *solution key* (solutions only):

```bash
quarto render Ch07-nonlin-lab.qmd --profile solution
```

The exercise/solution toggling is provided by the
[coatless-quarto/assign](https://github.com/coatless-quarto/assign)
Quarto extension.
